hero.title: Industrial design with curiosity, range, and purpose.
hero.body: I'm Jon Brooks, an industrial designer and design all-rounder who loves shaping thoughtful products, visuals, tools, and practical solutions.

workIntro: Industrial design work across furniture, homewares, lighting, and mobility, shaped through form, function, materials, visualisation, and practical problem-solving.

projects.title: Contour Lounge Chair
projects.materials: Dark olive upholstery, sculpted walnut frame, recessed support rails
projects.summary: An industrial design study in relaxed seating, built around a broad upholstered volume and a continuous timber frame. The chair balances comfort, structural clarity, and a distinct side profile without becoming visually heavy.
notes: The continuous side frame carries the seat, back, and rear support as one readable structure.
A reclined seat and broad cushions support a relaxed lounge posture.
Dark olive upholstery and walnut create a quiet, durable material contrast.

projects.title: Silhouette Sofa
projects.materials: Olive boucle upholstery, hardwood frame, blackened stone table
projects.summary: An exploration of soft seating as one continuous volume. The wraparound back, integrated arms, and low seat create a calm silhouette, while the paired table adds a grounded counterpoint.
notes: The continuous back and arms create a calm profile from every angle.
Dense upholstery texture holds shadow while softening the broad form.
The low table reinforces the sofa's horizontal proportion and everyday use.

projects.title: Arc Lounge Chair
projects.materials: Cream boucle, sculpted walnut, concealed webbing
projects.summary: A compact lounge chair developed around a continuous walnut arm rail and inset upholstered volumes. The design makes its construction legible while keeping the overall form light and welcoming.
notes: Rounded walnut transitions connect the arms and front legs without hard stops.
Generous cushion radii give the chair a softer domestic posture.
Contrasting upholstery and timber make the structure clear at a glance.

projects.title: Foundry Sideboard
projects.materials: Ribbed walnut, satin finish, slim timber legs
projects.summary: A storage product study combining a restrained cabinet volume with a finely ribbed walnut front. The fluting adds tactile depth, while inset legs keep the piece visually light in a living space.
notes: Vertical ribbing adds depth to the broad front without visual noise.
Inset legs lift the cabinet mass slightly above the floor.
A satin walnut finish retains grain detail while limiting glare.

projects.title: Ridge Dining Table
projects.materials: Dark walnut, satin finish, rounded bullnose top, four tapered corner legs
projects.summary: A dining table study focused on a softened rectangular top, honest four-leg construction, and proportions suited to everyday use. The bullnose edge and tapered legs give the solid walnut form a quieter presence.
notes: Rounded corners and a softened underside reduce the visual weight of the long top.
Four separate tapered legs provide a familiar and stable dining stance.
A satin walnut finish keeps the grain visible in warm interior light.

projects.title: Pivot Writing Desk
projects.materials: Dark walnut, powder-coated aluminium, wool felt, stainless steel fasteners
projects.summary: A compact dark-walnut writing desk organised around a charcoal service spine. Cable sockets, a sliding felt-lined tray, and a pivoting privacy panel make a small workstation useful without turning it into office equipment.
notes: The charcoal powder-coated aluminium service spine carries power, cable routing, and the sliding felt-lined pencil tray in one replaceable assembly.
A shallow pivoting walnut privacy panel screens visual clutter while preserving the desk's open proportions.
Bolted aluminium leg joints and separate stainless hardware let the desk flat-pack, move, and be repaired without damaging the walnut top.

projects.title: Flux Kettle
projects.materials: Satin warm-ivory vitreous enamel, graphite heat-safe polymer, brushed stainless steel, concealed stainless heating plate
projects.summary: A minimal electric kettle with a warm-ivory satin body, graphite grip and base, and a visible stainless hinge that makes the lid action immediately legible.
notes: The short brushed stainless spout controls flow while remaining visually separate from the hot enamelled body.
A graphite loop keeps the hand clear and places the stainless lid release naturally under the thumb.
An exposed stainless barrel hinge connects the lid through a visible pivot and stop, while the low graphite base carries one amber status light.

projects.title: Morrow Clock
projects.materials: Translucent tangerine recycled polycarbonate, mineral glass, plum dial, mint hands, cobalt anodised aluminium
projects.summary: A tactile analogue clock built around a thick translucent shell, a deeply recessed dial, and one direct control. Tangerine, plum, mint, and cobalt turn the layers of the assembly into a readable visual system.
notes: The translucent shell reveals case depth and protects the mineral-glass edge without a heavy bezel.
Mint hands and markers stay legible against the matte plum dial without relying on numerals.
One cobalt crown combines time and alarm adjustment while the warm-yellow indicator confirms state.

projects.title: Tessera Tray System
projects.materials: Sunflower, vermilion, and sky-blue recycled polymer, anodised aluminium rails, charcoal removable tray
projects.summary: A modular organisation system whose yellow, vermilion, charcoal, and blue components work as one stack or as separate item trays across a desk.
notes: Shared dimensions let every coloured module stack, separate, and move independently as work changes.
Exposed aluminium rails carry the load and create a consistent datum through the yellow, red, and blue parts.
The charcoal insert slides out as its own small-item tray for tools, stationery, or loose objects.

projects.title: Relay Radio
projects.materials: Forest-green powder-coated aluminium, off-white perforated steel, orange anodised aluminium, stainless antenna
projects.summary: A compact analogue radio with a forest-green shell, warm perforated grille, and two oversized orange controls. The design makes tuning, volume, and antenna storage obvious through touch and colour.
notes: The off-white perforated grille is a replaceable front component rather than a visual texture applied to the shell.
Two knurled orange controls use scale as well as colour to distinguish tuning from volume.
The stainless antenna folds into a recessed top channel while the chartreuse marker gives quiet analogue feedback.

projects.title: Torque Coffee Mill
projects.materials: Brushed, knurled, polished, and satin food-grade stainless steel
projects.summary: A manual coffee mill resolved entirely in stainless steel. Brushed bodies, a knurled grip, polished controls, and a satin faceted knob separate each interaction without relying on colour.
notes: The flared brushed-steel base widens the stance and separates as the removable grounds cup.
A broad knurled stainless band provides secure grip while the crank is under load.
The polished adjustment ring exposes calibration marks for repeatable settings and simple servicing.

projects.title: Silo Food Waste Caddy
projects.materials: Recycled polypropylene, brushed stainless steel, silicone, activated charcoal
projects.summary: A countertop food-waste caddy that keeps the parts people touch simple and the parts that get dirty removable. A sealed lid, lift-out bucket, and replaceable charcoal filter make daily composting cleaner.
notes: The stainless carry loop stays outside the waste volume and locks upright when the inner bucket is lifted out.
A silicone perimeter gasket and replaceable charcoal cassette control odour without hiding a complicated mechanism in the lid.
The recycled-polypropylene bucket, shell, filter carrier, and gasket separate without tools for cleaning and replacement.

projects.title: Cairn Table Lamp
projects.materials: Mouth-blown cobalt glass, amber cast glass, opal glass diffuser, brushed stainless dimmer
projects.summary: A sculptural table lamp built as a small cairn of mouth-blown glass. Cobalt, amber, and opal volumes stack without an exposed stem, turning the light source and dimmer into part of the composition.
notes: The thick cobalt base provides optical weight and a stable low centre of gravity.
An amber middle volume reveals the warm light source and carries a small side-mounted dimmer.
The broad opal dome diffuses light across the table while completing the balanced three-part stack.

projects.title: Vector Task Light
projects.materials: Laminated ash, vermilion powder-coated aluminium, ultramarine recycled terrazzo, stainless pivots, opal lens
projects.summary: An adjustable task light that makes its mechanics explicit through laminated ash arms, stainless pivots, a vermilion light bar, and an ultramarine terrazzo base.
notes: Parallel laminated-ash members keep the head controlled while adding warmth to the visible mechanism.
Large stainless pivot discs make the three adjustment points understandable and serviceable.
The terrazzo base counters the long red head and houses a direct metal control without visual clutter.

projects.title: Aperture Pendant
projects.materials: Translucent chartreuse recycled acrylic, opal silicone diffuser, braided vermilion textile, violet powder-coated aluminium
projects.summary: A thick translucent pendant that treats suspension as part of the colour composition. A chartreuse acrylic ring hangs from three vermilion textile cords beneath a compact violet canopy.
notes: The faceted recycled-acrylic ring gives the pendant visible thickness and refracts the inner light path.
Three braided cords provide stable suspension while carrying power without invisible secondary wires.
A removable opal inner diffuser softens the source and allows the LED assembly to be serviced.

projects.title: Rill Wall Light
projects.materials: Warm-grey powder-coated aluminium, brushed aluminium pivots, graphite controls, opal diffuser
projects.summary: A compact wall light built around a rotating cylindrical light engine. Turning the body redirects its recessed linear beam while the shallow hardwired plate keeps the mechanism close to the wall.
notes: The warm-grey extruded aluminium cylinder rotates between two compact machined pivots to aim the light up or down.
A recessed linear opal diffuser controls glare and disappears into the cylindrical body when the lamp is switched off.
The shallow rounded backplate conceals wiring and fixings, with a single flush graphite dimmer centred below the light.

projects.title: Trace Floor Lamp
projects.materials: Warm-grey flattened-oval aluminium, weighted steel, graphite polymer, brushed aluminium, opal diffuser
projects.summary: A full-scale floor reading lamp with a weighted racetrack base, gently leaning mast, and long tapered light blade. The extended reach places controlled light over a lounge chair without an articulated task-light frame.
notes: The flattened-oval aluminium mast leans forward slightly and carries concealed wiring from the weighted floor base.
A concealed clevis and small brushed pin let the 650 mm tapered blade tilt without a large exposed pivot disc.
The substantial racetrack base keeps the 1550 mm lamp stable while a flush rotary dimmer and recessed opal diffuser provide simple control.

projects.title: Beacon Portable Lantern
projects.materials: Bead-blasted aluminium, anodised aluminium, opal glass, brass, silicone
projects.summary: A rechargeable lantern whose light disc pivots inside a protective aluminium arch. It works as a table light, reading light, or low outdoor marker without changing parts or hiding its mechanism.
notes: Two small brass pivots let the opal light disc aim through a wide arc while the outer frame remains a stable carry handle.
A recessed rotary dimmer and protected USB-C port keep operation direct and the electrical parts replaceable.
The weighted silicone-footed base holds the lantern steady indoors while the sealed aluminium body tolerates everyday outdoor use.

projects.title: Stride Fold E-bike
projects.materials: Hydroformed aluminium, graphite engineering polymer, carbon belt, stainless steel, rubber
projects.summary: A compact 20-inch electric bicycle designed around a low-step frame and one secure central fold. Integrated lighting, a removable battery, and a clean commuter package make mixed train and street journeys easier to manage.
notes: The hydroformed low-step frame keeps the battery inside the downtube while leaving a clear and accessible step-through.
A compact clevis hinge, safety catch, and forest-green locking lever make the fold readable without dominating the frame.
A carbon belt, full guards, integrated lights, and a rear rack reduce maintenance and support daily all-weather travel.

projects.title: Aero Commuter Helmet
projects.materials: In-mould polycarbonate, recycled EPS, nylon webbing, soft-touch polymer, LED light guide
projects.summary: A low-profile urban helmet that combines deep continuous airflow channels with a compact fit system and integrated rear light. Its restrained shell is shaped for daily clothing rather than racing equipment.
notes: Three front-to-rear channels move air through the helmet while broad bridges maintain credible shell and liner structure.
The polycarbonate shell wraps the recycled-EPS edge to protect high-contact areas and give the lower profile a clean datum.

A central red running and brake light, paired amber turn indicators, synced wearable wrist indicators, a conventional three-prong side-release buckle, and an indexed occipital dial make visibility and fit simple to manage.

projects.title: Latch Convertible Pannier
projects.materials: TPU-coated recycled nylon, thermoformed recycled polymer, anodised aluminium, nylon webbing
projects.summary: A weather-resistant bicycle pannier that leaves its rack hardware behind visually when carried off the bike. A structured back panel, concealed release, and stowable shoulder strap support a clean transition into work or home.
notes: The welded recycled-nylon body uses a roll-top inner closure beneath a broad weather flap secured by two low-profile compression clasps.
Two compact spring-loaded upper hooks wrap the rack's side rail and release together through one recessed green pull.
A lower anti-sway catch locks around the rack stay, holding the bag clear of the wheel and heel while the shoulder strap remains fully stowed.

projects.title: Gauge Electric Pump
projects.materials: Die-cast aluminium, graphite engineering polymer, braided nylon, rubber, lithium-ion cells
projects.summary: A compact rechargeable electric tyre pump with a stored braided hose, clear pressure display, and one rotary push control. An LED built into the end of the nozzle illuminates the valve exactly where it is needed.
notes: The die-cast aluminium shell works as both a durable enclosure and a heat spreader around the compressor assembly.
The single braided hose stores in a recessed front channel; when deployed, that channel is empty and the hose exits directly toward the tyre.
A focused LED ring sits only at the nozzle tip, while a service door, replaceable seals, and exposed fasteners support repair.

projects.title: Rove Carry-on
projects.materials: Recycled polycarbonate, anodised aluminium, TPE, recycled nylon, stainless steel
projects.summary: A compact cabin case designed around the parts that wear first. Recessed handles, replaceable wheel cassettes, and separate corner bumpers keep the shell quiet and make damage straightforward to repair.
notes: Four wheel cassettes release from inside the case so worn wheels can be replaced without cutting or drilling the shell.
A low-profile telescopic handle sits behind the packing volume and locks through a broad, comfortable aluminium grip.
Separate TPE corner bumpers take impacts at transfer points and can be replaced independently of the recycled-polycarbonate body.

projects.title: Arc Hinged D-Lock
projects.materials: Hardened steel, bead-blasted aluminium, moulded elastomer, stainless steel
projects.summary: A compact hinged D-lock built as one captive assembly. A permanent pivot keeps the shackle attached, while a recessed receiver and separate frame mount make locking and daily carry easy to understand.
notes: The blue-sleeved hardened-steel shackle stays permanently captured by a concealed permanent pivot inside the bead-blasted aluminium crossbar, so unlocking never creates a loose part.
The free end swings into a recessed receiver with a stainless-steel collar and an internal deadbolt; the recessed key cylinder and cool-white LED ring sit beside it.
A separate low-profile frame mount is only a transport accessory: rubber-lined guides hold the closed lock vertically and a thumb tab releases its spring latch.

views.label: Closed lock releasing from frame mount
Frame and rear wheel secured to a street rail
Shackle closing around frame and fixed rack
Closed lock stored with cycling gear

about.title: About
about.body: I'm an industrial designer and design all-rounder who genuinely enjoys shaping products, furniture, graphics, and digital tools. I'm self-taught in graphic design, with experience creating catalogues, production artwork, and supporting marketing material. I also have strong hands-on experience with AI and image generation, using it as one part of a wider design process. I value other people's experience, ask questions, and make a steady effort to learn, upskill, and improve my work.
about.experienceIntro: A practical mix of industrial design, furniture, graphic communication, image-making, and the curiosity to keep learning.

experience.description: Taking products and furniture from early ideas through CAD, materials, visualisation, detailing, and production-ready outcomes.
Experience in creating catalogues, layouts, production artwork, and supporting marketing material. With guidance from the Marketing team.
Using AI image generation as a practical part of concept development to explore form, colour, materials, and context while keeping design judgement at the centre.
I value the experience and perspective of other people. I ask questions, stay open to better ways of working, and put in the time to learn, upskill, and improve wherever a project asks more of me.
I enjoy unfamiliar problems and practical constraints, and work to find clear solutions without losing the character of the original idea.

contact.title: Contact
contact.body: For commissions, collaborations, or industrial design work, send a note with the product, scope, timeline, and any practical constraints.
